description: UK referendum on EU membership. Leave vs Remain. Leave won 51.9% with turnout 72.2%. Tests late-surge dynamics, shy voter effects, immigration salience, and elite endorsement impact.

key_events.description: Cameron announces EU deal and sets referendum date. Slight Remain boost.
Boris Johnson declares for Leave. Major credibility boost, polls narrow ~2pts.
Obama visits London, warns UK would be 'back of the queue' for trade deal.
ONS releases net migration figure of 333,000 (near record). Leave surges on immigration.
ITV EU referendum debate. Farage's 'breaking point' poster. Leave consolidation.
Murder of Jo Cox MP by far-right extremist. Polls swing ~3-4pts toward Remain temporarily.

calibration_notes: Tests late-surge dynamics and shy voter effects. Polls underestimated Leave by 2-4pts. Key: immigration salience driving shifts, elite endorsement effects (Johnson), exogenous shock (Cox murder) causing temporary reversal, undecideds breaking for change option, diminishing returns on fear-based messaging.
